items.title: Conserved heavy/light contacts and germline preferences revealed by a large-scale analysis of natively paired human antibody sequences and structural data
items.author: Konrad Krawczyk
items.content: Data mining of large paired heavy-light antibody sequences reveals preferences in germline usage and conserved inter chain contact residues.

items.title: Repression via DNA looping by the Gram-positive global transcriptional regulator ScoC from Geobacillus
items.author: Gil Shoham
items.content: The structure of the Gram-Positive global regulator ScoC complexed with DNA reveals that it can bind two distinct DNA sites and induce DNA looping. DNA recognition occurs via the minor groove and both ScoC binding sites are required for repression.

items.title: EEG neural indicator of temporal integration in the human auditory brain with clinical implications
items.author: Xiongjie Yu
items.content: EEG and behavioral data reveal how the human brain merges rapid sounds into unified percepts, identifying neural markers of temporal integration with implications for clinical assessment of consciousness.

items.title: Single-cell antigen receptor sequencing in pigs with influenza
items.author: John P. Driver
items.content: We developed porcine-specific primers for TCR and BCR, enabling paired single-cell RNA and receptor sequencing of pig T and B cells. Using this assay, we tracked treatment-induced changes in TCR and BCR repertoires in pig lung tissue.

items.title: Author Correction: The lifelong nonlinear development of spatial variability of brain signals
items.author: Georg Northoff
items.content: Author Correction: The lifelong nonlinear development of spatial variability of brain signals

items.title: Pericyte-mediated regulation of angiogenesis during cutaneous wound healing in adult zebrafish
items.author: Shigetomo Fukuhara
items.content: Live imaging in adult zebrafish reveals that pericytes actively regulate wound angiogenesis by restricting endothelial cell proliferation, sprouting, and directional migration, thereby ensuring the formation of a well-organized vascular network.

items.title: Publisher Correction: IL-33/ST2 drives inflammatory pain via CCL2 signaling and activation of TRPV1 and TRPM8
items.author: Junting Huang
items.content: Publisher Correction: IL-33/ST2 drives inflammatory pain via CCL2 signaling and activation of TRPV1 and TRPM8
